steps.title: Check current falco deployment
steps.description:
Verify your falco version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
helm list -n falco 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working falco installation.

steps.title: Review falco configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant falco configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
kubectl get configmap -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=falco
```
Hey,

Can you guys release ARM64 builds? I mainly run Kubernetes on ~50-100 ARM clusters and wanted to test Falco out.

steps.title: Apply the fix for ARM64 Build
steps.description:
Adding some files to the build dir to make it easier to build Falco.
Right now checking out the repository on Linux is a bit confusing.
Now the cmake flags are defined in a convenience scripts /build/init.sh
So they can be easily switched while building.

Note that the true changes needed were
```yaml
[novix@novix build]$ pacaur -S linux-headers
:: There are 16 providers available for linux-headers:
:: Repository core
   1) linux-am33x-headers  2) linux-armv7-headers  3) linux-armv7-rc-headers  4) linux-clearfog-headers  5) linux-cubox-headers
   6) linux-headers-odroid  7) linux-headers-utilite-dt  8) linux-imx6-headers  9) linux-odroid-c1-headers  10) linux-odroid-xu3-headers
   11) linux-peach-headers  12) linux-raspberrypi-headers  13) linux-raspberrypi4-headers  14) linux-utilite-headers
   15) linux-veyron-headers  16) linux-zedboard-headers
```

steps.title: Upgrade falco to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade falco:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade falco falco/falco --namespace falco
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falco
helm list -n falco
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
kubectl get events -n falco --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "ARM64 Build" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Adding some files to the build dir to make it easier to build Falco.
Right now checking out the repository on Linux is a bit confusing.
Now the cmake flags are defined in a convenience scripts /build/init.sh
So they can be easily switched while building.

Note that the true changes needed were mostly cosmetic and related to dependencies with Falco.
codeSnippets:
[novix@novix build]$ pacaur -S linux-headers
:: There are 16 providers available for linux-headers:
:: Repository core
   1) linux-am33x-headers  2) linux-armv7-headers  3) linux-armv7-rc-headers  4) linux-clearfog-headers  5) linux-cubox-headers
   6) linux-headers-odroid  7) linux-headers-utilite-dt  8) linux-imx6-headers  9) linux-odroid-c1-headers  10) linux-odroid-xu3-headers
   11) linux-peach-headers  12) linux-raspberrypi-headers  13) linux-raspberrypi4-headers  14) linux-utilite-headers
   15) linux-veyron-headers  16) linux-zedboard-headers

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright (C) 2019 The Falco Authors.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
# the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
# http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software distributed under the License is distributed on an
# "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the
# specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.
#


# @kris-nova We have experimental support for ARM and I have tested
# on armv7. Please use this flag as experimental only.
BUILD_ARM=true


cmake ../ \
      -DBUILD_BPF=OFF \
      -DBUILD_WARNINGS_AS_ERRO

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with falco installed or the issue environment reproducible.
